revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|qid=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}, Higashiishikiri-chō 1-1-1 (Main Shrine)
{{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|qid=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}, Ueishikiri-chō 2 (Kamino-sha) | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|55.9|N|135|38|46.5|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10949054}}, Kami-musubi-no-mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small), Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Traditional date: 2nd year of Emperor Jimmu (659 BCE) | Festivals = April 15–16 (Spring Grand Festival)
August 3–4 (Summer Grand Festival)
October 21–22 (Autumn Grand Festival) | Other names = Ishikiri-san
"God of Tumors" | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ishikiritsurugiya-jinja Higashiosaka Osaka pref Japan05n.jpg|270px]]
Main Shrine | Map = {{Switcher | {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|lat_deg=34|lat_min=40|lat_sec=55.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.5|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=230|caption=}} | location within Osaka Prefecture | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=230|coord={{Coord|34|40|55.9|N|135|38|46.5|E}}}} }} }} [[File:Ishikiri Tsurugiya-Jinja kaminomiya1.jpg|thumb|220px|Kamino-sha]] '''Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine''' (石切劔箭神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|qid=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is affiliated with {{ill|Shintō Ishikiri Sect|ja|神道石切教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Ishikiri Sect|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Kawachi District. The shrine is locally known as "Ishikiri-san" or "The God of Tumors." == History == The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Ohyakudo-mairi|ja|お百度参り|1=Hyakudo Mairi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohyakudo-mairi|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakudo Mairi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百度参り}}, a ritual involving repetitive prayer walks between two sacred stones. Its precise founding date is unclear due to a fire that destroyed most records and the treasure repository. However, the Kizumi family, hereditary shrine caretakers, attribute the origin to the 2nd year of Emperor Jimmu's reign (659 BCE) when Kami-musubi-no-mikoto enshrined Nigi-hayahi at Mount Miyama. During Emperor Sujin's reign, the shrine was relocated to its current site. The shrine appears in historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, which notes that in 865 (Jōgan 7), it was conferred the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine faced significant destruction during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} due to conflict, including the burning of its Buddhist temple, {{ill|Hōtsū-ji|ja|法通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtsū-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was later rebuilt, and its rituals were restored, but many original features were moved to the current Kamino-sha. In the modern period, it was classified as a village shrine. In 1905 (Meiji 38), Nigi-hayahi was officially enshrined in the main sanctuary. Post-war, the shrine established the Shintō Ishikiri Sect as its religious headquarters. A branch shrine, "Ishikiri Tokyo Bunshi," was founded in 1975 in Tokyo. == Deities == * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日尊|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigi-hayahi-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|可美真手命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウマシマジ}} (Kami-musubi-no-mikoto), the son of Nigi-hayahi. These are collectively referred to as "Ishikiri Daimyōjin." == festivals == * '''October 22 – Autumn Grand Festival''': A ceremony of gratitude for the year's harvest, featuring the purification ritual "Miyu Kagura." During the festival, the shrine treasures are displayed to the public. * '''December 31 – Year-End Purification Ceremony''': Participants cleanse sins and impurities through rituals like {{ill|Chinowa-kuguri|ja|茅の輪くぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinowa-kuguri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=Ōharae-shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11261910}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Tachi Sword ("Ishikiri-maru")''' – Forged by {{ill|Sanjō Yukiie|ja|三条有成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjō Yukiie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Osaka City Natural Monuments === * '''Camphor Tree''' at Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine. == Access == * '''Main Shrine''': 7 minutes' walk from {{ill|Shin-Ishikiri Station|en|Shin-Ishikiri Station|qid=Q7497096|lt=Shin-Ishikiri Station|1=Shin-Ishikiri Station}} (Kintetsu Keihanna Line) or 15 minutes from {{ill|Ishikiri Station|en|Ishikiri Station|qid=Q6080325|lt=Ishikiri Station|1=Ishikiri Station}} (Kintetsu Nara Line). * '''Kamino-sha''': 7 minutes' walk from Ishikiri Station, about 1 km east of the Main Shrine. == External Links == * [http://www.ishikiri.or.jp/ Official Website of Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine] {{Isonokami Faith}} {{translated page|ja|石切剣箭神社|version=105523234|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine (Q11585189)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Kawachi district|ja|河内郡 (大阪府)|zh|河內郡 (大阪府)|qid=Q11553458|1=Kawachi district}} || {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|lt=Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|commons|Category:Ishikiri Tsurugiya-Jinja|qid=Q11585189|1=Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|qid=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || 3 seats || — || {{coord|34.682138|135.646238|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180915 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11585189}}==Notes== [[ja:石切剣箭神社]] [[Category:Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
